units.title: My family
units.subtitle: Meet your grandparents and cousins, and learn to describe the people in your photos.
units.grammar_focus: Possessive 's (Minh's school) and possessive pronouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs).

spiral_meta.grammar_anticipation: there is / there are — 2-3 passive exposures in passages (Unit 3 will teach explicitly)

lessons.title: Grandparents and cousins
lessons.topic: Extended family — grandparents, sons, daughters, cousins, twins; possessive 's

steps.instruction: Linh shows Ray a family tree. Watch and answer.

questions.correct_answer: her mum's parents and her dad's parents
options: her mum's parents and her dad's parents

questions.question_text: What is the word for two children born on the same day to the same mum?

items.definition: Your parent's mother or father.
items.example: My grandparents live near a river.

items.definition: Your mum's or dad's father.
items.example: My grandfather is called Ông Hai.

items.definition: Your mum's or dad's mother.
items.example: My grandmother makes the best soup.

items.definition: A boy child of a mother and father.
A girl child of a mother and father.
Your son's or daughter's boy child.
Your son's or daughter's girl child.
Your uncle's or aunt's child.
items.example: My uncle has two sons — they are my cousins.

items.definition: One of two children born on the same day to the same mother.

games.instruction: Match each picture with the right word.
Listen to the word and tap the right picture.
Look at the picture and type the word.

steps.title: Linh's grandparents
steps.text: My grandparents live in a small town near a long river. My grandfather is called Ông Hai. He is my dad's father. My grandmother is called Bà Mai. She is my mum's mother. I am their granddaughter and my brother Minh is their grandson. We visit them in summer. My uncle Quan has two sons — they are my cousins. They are twins! Their bedroom has two beds, two desks and two big windows. The kitchen at my grandparents' house is small but it smells of soup all day.
steps.scene_description: A warm scene of Vietnamese grandparents — Ông Hai with kind eyes, Bà Mai in a simple ao bà ba — sitting on a wooden porch of a small house near a river, with two identical 8-year-old twin boys playing in the garden.

questions.question: How many cousins does Linh have?
Where do Linh's grandparents live?
questions.answer: in a small town near a river
options: in a small town near a river
in a village near the sea
questions.correct_answer: in a small town near a river

steps.explanation: To say something belongs to a person, we add 's after their name or role. For two or more people, we add only the apostrophe ' after the plural: my parents' house. There is a sister bigger structure — there is/there are — that we will learn in Unit 3, but you will see it in our reading today, too.
examples: My grandmother's soup is the best.
My uncle's two sons are twins.
My parents' house is in Ho Chi Minh City.
There is a small garden at my grandparents' house. (Unit 3 will explain 'there is' fully.)

items.sentence: My ___ house is in Ho Chi Minh City.
___ bedroom is on the first floor.
My ___ house is in Ho Chi Minh City.
___ bedroom is on the first floor.
My grandmother's soup is the best.
My uncle's two sons am twins.
My parents' house is in Ho Chi Minh City.
There am a small garden at my grandparents' house. (Unit 3 will explain 'there is' fully.)

steps.audio_text: Linh: Ray, look — this is a photo of my grandparents. Ray: Lovely! What are they called? Linh: My grandfather is called Ông Hai. My grandmother is called Bà Mai. Ray: And the two boys playing in the garden? Linh: They are my cousins. They are twins! My uncle Quan is their father. They live in the same town as my grandparents. Ray: So you are your grandparents' granddaughter, and Minh is their grandson, right? Linh: Yes, that's right!

questions.question: How many cousins does Linh have?

steps.prompt: Tell Ray about your grandparents or cousins. Use 's where you can.
steps.expected_text: My grandfather/grandmother is called [name]. My grandparents live in [place]. I have [number] cousin(s). My cousin's name is [name].
prompts: Say your grandfather's or grandmother's name. ('My ___ is called ___.')
Say where your grandparents live. ('My grandparents live in ___.')
Say how many cousins you have. ('I have ___ cousins.')

questions.question: Choose: My ___ live near a river.
Two kids born on the same day are ___.

lessons.title: What do they look like?
lessons.topic: Describing people — tall, short, young, old, blonde, curly, fair, thin, fat; possessive pronouns intro

steps.instruction: Ray shows you photos of the class. Watch and answer.

questions.question_text: Which word means 'with light yellow hair'?
questions.hint: Sophie has this kind of hair.

items.definition: Higher than average from head to feet.
Not very high — the opposite of tall.
Not many years old.
items.example: Minh is young — he is only seven.

items.definition: Many years old — the opposite of young.
items.example: My grandfather is old but very kind.

items.definition: With light yellow hair.
Going round in small circles — used for hair.
Light in colour — for skin or hair.
items.example: Sophie has fair hair and fair skin.

items.definition: Not fat — with a small body.
items.example: My cousin is thin and quick.

items.definition: With a big body — the opposite of thin.
items.example: Our cat is a little bit fat.

games.instruction: 60 seconds! Tap the right picture for each word.
Read the word and tap the right picture.
Flip the cards and find the pairs.

steps.title: My online class — what we look like
steps.text: I love my online class. We are all different. Tom is tall for nine. He is American and his hair is short and dark. Sophie is from London. She is fair and her hair is blonde and a bit curly. She is not very tall. Lucas is Brazilian. He has curly black hair and big brown eyes. He is thin and very quick when we play. My best friend Mai is short and young — like me. We are all nine, but we look very different. Our class is like a big family on the internet.
steps.scene_description: A photo-style grid of six 9-year-old kids' portraits, each showing different hair colour and texture: a Vietnamese girl with dark straight hair, a tall American boy, a British girl with blonde curly hair, a Brazilian boy with curly dark hair, and two more Vietnamese kids. Natural daylight.

questions.question: How old are the kids in the class?

steps.rule_pattern: Possessive pronouns — mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs
steps.explanation: Possessive pronouns replace 'my/your/his/her/our/their + noun'. We don't say 'this is my pen' twice; we say 'This pen is mine.' Hers, his and ours work the same way. We will use 'there is/there are' a little in our reading today — that's coming in Unit 3.
examples: This bag is mine. (= my bag)
That book is yours. (= your book)
The pink pencil case is hers.
There is a big difference: the twins' room is theirs, not ours.

items.sentence: The blonde girl's hair is her.
This bag am mine. (= my bag)
This bag is mine. (= my bag)
That book am yours. (= your book)
The pink pencil case am hers.
There am a big difference: the twins' room is theirs, not ours.

steps.audio_text: Ray: Class, look at the photos on your screen. Tom: Ray, I am the tall boy with short dark hair. Ray: Yes — that one is yours. Tom: And this blonde girl — who is she? Sophie: That one is mine! I am the blonde girl with curly hair. Lucas: And the one with very curly black hair is mine. I am Lucas. I am thin and quick! Linh: My photo is the one with the short Vietnamese girl. That photo is mine. My brother Minh is young — only seven. Ray: Excellent class! All your photos are different and all of them are amazing.

questions.question: Which photo is Tom's?
questions.answer: the tall boy with short dark hair
options: the tall boy with short dark hair
the blonde girl with curly hair
the boy with very curly black hair
questions.correct_answer: the tall boy with short dark hair

steps.prompt: Describe yourself or a friend with at least 3 adjectives from this lesson.
steps.expected_text: I am [tall/short/young/old]. My hair is [blonde/dark/curly/straight/fair]. I am [thin/fat]. My best friend is also [adjective].
prompts: Say if you are tall or short.
Say about your hair (colour or curly/straight).
Say if you are young or old (you are young!).
End with one possessive pronoun. ('This [thing] is mine.')

questions.question: Sophie has yellow hair. She is ___.
Lucas has curly black hair. His hair is ___.
True or false: 'These photos are ours.' is correct.

lessons.title: My family photo
lessons.topic: Faces — moustache, beard, hair, eyes; possessive pronouns in action

steps.instruction: Linh shows Ray her family photo. Watch and answer.

questions.question_text: Which word means 'short hair on a man's top lip'?
Which word means 'hair on a man's face — chin and cheeks'?

items.definition: Short hair on a man's top lip.
items.example: My uncle has a thin moustache.

items.definition: Hair on a man's chin and cheeks.
items.example: My grandfather has a small white beard.

items.definition: What grows on your head.
What you see with — you have two.
A happy mouth shape — turned up at the corners.
items.example: My grandmother has a kind smile.

items.definition: Used to ask about appearance: 'What does she look like?'
A picture you take with a phone or camera.
Friendly and nice to other people.

games.instruction: Listen to the word, then type it.
Listen and tap the right word.

steps.title: Our family photo on the wall
steps.text: On the wall in my bedroom there is a family photo. My grandfather has a small white beard and kind eyes. My grandmother has short fair hair and a beautiful smile. My dad has a thin moustache. He looks like my grandfather, but he is younger. My mum has long dark hair. People say I look like her. Minh looks like my dad — he has the same big eyes. My cousins, the twins, look like each other! It is hard to know whose photo is whose. This photo is ours, and we love it. In the kitchen we have one more family photo, with our aunt and uncle, too.
steps.scene_description: A photo-realistic close-up of a wooden picture frame on a soft pink bedroom wall. Inside the frame: a multi-generation Vietnamese family portrait — grandfather with white beard, grandmother with short fair hair and a warm smile, parents (mum with long dark hair, dad with thin moustache), 9-year-old daughter and 7-year-old son, plus two identical twin boys at the side.

questions.question: Where is the family photo?
questions.answer: on the wall in Linh's bedroom
options: on the wall in Linh's bedroom
questions.correct_answer: on the wall in Linh's bedroom

questions.question: Who has a small white beard?

steps.explanation: To talk about appearance: 'X looks like Y.' or 'X has Y's eyes/smile/nose.' Mix this with possessive pronouns: 'This photo is ours.' Notice that we will say 'there is a photo on the wall' fully in Unit 3 — for today, just use 'is' as in 'There is a family photo.'
examples: My grandfather has a small white beard.
Whose photo is this? — It's mine.
There is a family photo on the wall. (Full 'there is/are' in Unit 3.)

items.sentence: My grandfather has a small white ___.
There ___ a family photo on the wall. (Full 'there is/are' in Unit 3.)
There ___ a family photo on the wall. (Full 'there is/are' in Unit 3.)
Whose photo am this? — It's mine.
There am a family photo on the wall. (Full 'there is/are' in Unit 3.)
Whose photo am this? — It's mine.
There am a family photo on the wall. (Full 'there is/are' in Unit 3.)

steps.audio_text: Ray: Linh, can you describe the people in your family photo? Linh: Of course. My grandfather has a small white beard and kind eyes. My grandmother has short fair hair and a beautiful smile. Ray: Does your dad have a beard, too? Linh: No, he has a thin moustache. He looks like my grandfather, but he is younger. Ray: And your mum? Linh: My mum has long dark hair. People say I look like her. Ray: What about Minh? Linh: Minh looks like my dad. He has big eyes, like my dad. Ray: Lovely photo! And this photo is yours, of course. Linh: Yes, this one is mine — well, it's ours.

questions.question: Does Linh's dad have a beard?
What is Linh's grandmother's smile like?

steps.prompt: Describe one person in your family photo. Use 2-3 face words and one 'look like' sentence.
steps.expected_text: My [person] has [hair/eyes/beard/moustache/smile]. He/She is [tall/short/young/old]. I look like [person].
prompts: Say who the person is. ('My grandfather/dad/mum/aunt is in my photo.')
Say one face detail. ('He/She has a ___ / fair hair / big eyes.')

questions.question: Hair under a man's nose is a ___.
Hair on a man's chin and cheeks is a ___.
True or false: 'My grandmother has a kind smile.' is correct.

lessons.title: My family — Review
lessons.topic: Cumulative review: extended family, descriptions, possessive 's + possessive pronouns

steps.instruction: Review time! Ray asks you to remember the unit. Answer 2 quick questions.

questions.question_text: Which grammar did we practise the most?

games.instruction: Climb the ladder! Choose the right word for each step.
90 seconds! Race through all the words you learned.

steps.title: Our big family photo
steps.text: This is my big family photo. There are eleven people in it. My grandparents are at the back. My grandfather has a small white beard and kind eyes. My grandmother has short fair hair and a beautiful smile. My dad has a thin moustache. He looks like my grandfather, but he is younger. My mum has long dark hair. I look like her. Minh, my brother, is seven and very young. He looks like Dad. Our uncle Quan is tall and a little bit fat. His sons are my cousins — they are twins! They are both thin and short. We also have one big family rule: when we eat in the kitchen, everyone helps. The photo is ours, and it lives near the door of my bedroom. We are not a big family by Vietnamese standards, but for me, this family is the best.
steps.scene_description: A wide photo-realistic family portrait: three generations of a Vietnamese family standing in front of a small traditional house. Grandparents in the centre (grandfather with white beard, grandmother with fair short hair), parents on the left (dad with moustache, mum with long dark hair), 9-year-old daughter and 7-year-old son, plus aunt + uncle + twin cousins. Natural daylight, warm tones.

questions.question: How many people are in the family photo?
Where does the photo live?
questions.answer: near the door of Linh's bedroom
options: near the door of Linh's bedroom
questions.correct_answer: near the door of Linh's bedroom

patterns: Possessive 's: Minh's bag, my parents' house, grandfather's beard
Possessive pronouns: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs
look like: Minh looks like Dad. I look like my mum.
Descriptive be sentences: She is tall. Her hair is curly.

items.sentence: Possessive 's: Minh's bag, my parents' house, grandfather's beard
Possessive pronouns: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs
look like: Minh looks like Dad. I look like my mum.
Descriptive be sentences: She am tall. Her hair is curly.
Descriptive be sentences: She ___ tall. Her hair is curly.
Descriptive be sentences: She ___ tall. Her hair is curly.

steps.audio_text: Ray: Class, today is family-photo day. Linh, start! Linh: Hi everyone. This is my big family photo. My grandfather has a white beard and a kind smile. My grandmother has short fair hair. My dad has a thin moustache and my mum has long dark hair. I look like my mum and Minh looks like Dad. My cousins are twins — they are thin and short. Ray: Tom, what about you? Tom: My family is small. I have one little sister. She is blonde and very young. I am tall and my hair is short. Ray: Sophie? Sophie: My family is small, too. My grandparents are in London. My grandmother has curly fair hair like mine. We look like each other. Ray: Lovely! Each photo is yours and it's special.

steps.prompt: Tell Ray about your whole family in 6-8 sentences. Use 's, possessive pronouns and 'look like' at least once each.
steps.expected_text: My family is [big/small]. My grandfather/grandmother has [feature]. My dad/mum is [adjective]. I have [number] brother/sister/cousin(s). I look like my [person]. This photo is mine/ours.
prompts: Say if your family is big or small.
Say if you have cousins, brothers or sisters.
End with a possessive pronoun. ('This photo is ___.')

questions.question: Choose: My ___ live near a river.
Two kids born on the same day are ___.
Sophie has yellow hair. She is ___.
Hair under a man's nose is a ___.
